site.metaTitle: AFOR — Facilities for mining and industry | Architecture and Engineering
site.metaDescription: Chilean firm specialized in architecture, engineering and project management for the development of facilities for large-scale mining and industry. Over 10 years of experience with large-scale mining clients and Tier 1 engineering firms.

hero.summary: We are a Chilean firm specialized in assets for non-process infrastructure for mining and industrial operations. We bring more than a decade of team experience to projects in the sector.

stripe.lineTwo: of facilities for mining and industry.

about.p1: We develop facilities for mining and industry: offices, change houses, control rooms, electrical and technical rooms, truck shops, hazardous waste storage and warehousing areas. We integrate architecture, engineering and multidisciplinary coordination under a single technical direction.
about.p2: We embed within the project team as an extension of your engineering. We adopt the client's coding, manuals and delivery standards, and operate under their internal protocols to ensure deliverables compatible with their document management system.
about.p3: The team that takes the project from technical surveying is the same one that coordinates the final delivery. Complete technical continuity, with no internal handoffs or intermediaries.
side: We work embedded in the client project team. No intermediaries, no internal transfers.
We approach each facility from an integrated view: architecture, engineering, BIM coordination and code, under a single technical direction.
We guarantee complete technical continuity and compatibility with the client document system.

principles.p1Body: Each facility responds to the logic of the production process: operations, personnel flow, safety and service continuity.
principles.p2Body: Mastery of regulations and safety standards for large-scale mining and industry. Complete documentation for approvals.
principles.p3Body: We work in Revit/BIM with the coding your project requires. Coordinated models, free of interferences.
principles.p4Body: Dedicated team for the project, no overhead. Aligned with your processes, manuals and schedules.

projects.subtitle: A representative selection of projects in large-scale mining and industry.

list.title: CHO Building Remediation
list.body: Comprehensive remediation of 420 m² to convert the CHO building into a high-availability technical facility. Internal layout, accesses, technical room for electrical equipment and UPS, offices, cowork areas and architectural improvements in ceilings, furniture and auxiliary systems.

list.title: Autonomous Trucks Facilities
list.body: Design of supporting facilities for the Autonomous Trucks program at a major mining operation. Drilling Control Room with raised technical floor, wood finishes, multiscreen monitoring stations, precision climate control and regulatory signage. Interim engineering ready for construction.

list.title: Mine Operations Control Room
list.body: Complete intervention with linear metal ceiling, wooden batten wall finish, recessed linear LED lighting and ergonomic distribution of operator stations with articulated arms and multi-screen monitors. Design that prioritizes operational concentration, acoustic comfort and luminous control for extended shifts.

list.title: Industrial Control Room
list.body: New operations control room featuring melamine wall claddings and aluminum trims. Linear U-profile ceiling with integrated lighting, engineered for acoustic performance and visual comfort across the shifts the operation requires. Layout centered on operator consoles with clean circulation paths, dimmable lighting control, and robust finishes designed for continuous industrial use.

list.title: Civic District and Infrastructure
list.body: Architectural design of the civic district and supporting infrastructure for a major mining project. Definition of administrative offices, access control, restrooms and change rooms with modular distribution oriented to optimize circulation flows and regulatory compliance. Site layouts, parking and vehicle accesses integrated with the general site plan.

list.title: Industrial and Corporate Building
list.body: Architectural design of an industrial and corporate building for an industrial client. Industrial warehouse with integrated administrative offices, translucent polycarbonate facade with exposed metallic structure, differentiated vehicle and pedestrian accesses, perimeter landscaping and parking.

list.title: Corporate Offices
list.body: Fit-out of new corporate offices for a professional services firm. Elegant and modern design with natural wood furniture, exposed ceiling with linear LED lighting, metal shelves with integrated vegetation as room dividers and collaborative workstations. Priority on natural light, warmth of materials and open distribution.

list.title: Restrooms — University Infrastructure
list.body: End-to-end project management of a restroom facility built for the relentless rhythm of campus life: thousands of daily uses, intensive cleaning cycles, and a service life measured in decades. Modular partitions in graphite gray, cementitious wall claddings, heavy-duty porcelain flooring, and an exposed steel frame painted matte black that organizes circulation without concealing the architecture. Recessed LED lighting at neutral color temperature, integrated accessible signage, and a suite of construction details —skirtings, ventilation grilles, ironmongery— engineered to withstand the most demanding use while holding institutional-grade finish.

list.title: Technical Roof — Data Center
list.body: End-to-end project management for the replacement of the technical roof over a 600 m² institutional server room. New bay-registrable structure engineered to host precision equipment that tolerates no pauses and no leaks: constant HVAC, electrical redundancy, and a 24/7 operation that cannot be stopped during construction. Pre-painted metal profiles, underlay membrane, thermal sealing, and a work plan coordinated with the datacenter shift schedule —each panel installed without disconnecting a single rack— to intervene the institution's digital core while the machine keeps running.

projects.heading: Featured projects

services.heading: Services
services.intro: Three areas covering the full lifecycle of a facility: design it, transform it, and keep it running.
transversal: BIM · Revit under the client's coding standards

areas.title: New-build projects
areas.tagline: Facilities from scratch: from plot plan to detail engineering, ready for construction.

subs.title: Plot plans and facility master plans
subs.body: Layouts, architectural program, operational flows and a master plan coordinated with structural and MEP disciplines.

subs.title: Administrative offices and change houses
subs.body: Spaces for personnel: technical offices, meeting rooms, dining halls, locker rooms and restrooms under mining regulations.

subs.title: Control rooms, server and technical rooms
subs.body: Critical rooms with raised floors, precision climate control, lighting and acoustic control and ergonomic operator stations.

subs.title: Hazardous waste, storage and truck shops
subs.body: Technical buildings for hazardous waste, industrial warehouses and heavy-equipment workshops with certified operational safety.

areas.title: Interior remodeling
areas.tagline: Workspaces in mining and industry, transformed without stopping the operation.

subs.title: Full workspace remodeling
subs.body: Offices, dining halls, change houses and common areas in existing installations, renewed end to end.

subs.title: Fit-out and change of use
subs.body: Conversion of buildings and rooms for new operational programs, with a complete regulatory dossier.

subs.title: Standard upgrades in operation
subs.body: Improved finishes, comfort and regulatory compliance with works planned around the operation's shifts.

areas.title: Critical facility maintenance
areas.tagline: Engineering for existing infrastructure that cannot fail or stop.

subs.title: Roofing and technical envelopes
subs.body: Replacement of roofs, membranes and seals over operating rooms — including data centers and 24/7 technical rooms.

subs.title: Industrial cladding and finishes
subs.body: Renewal of interior and exterior cladding with industrial-grade, high-traffic materials.

subs.title: Technical repairs and replacements
subs.body: Assessment and repair engineering for compromised structural and construction elements.

contact.body: Tell us about the project: scope, deadlines and client specifications. We reply within 24 business hours.
